blocks.speaker: Wren
blocks.content: I was by the water. The whole afternoon. I had my book.

blocks.speaker: Wren
blocks.content: I went inside once for water. I was back in two minutes.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: Did anyone see you at the water the whole time?

blocks.speaker: Wren
blocks.content: Nadia was on the beach. She could see me from where she was sitting.

blocks.speaker: Nadia
blocks.content: I could see the water from where I was. I wasn't watching Wren specifically.

blocks.speaker: Wren
blocks.content: That's fine. I'm just saying I was there.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: Nadia. You stayed on the beach.

blocks.speaker: Nadia
blocks.content: Yes. I was there when Cade left and when he came back. There when Sophie came out in clean clothes. There when Dean made his trips to the kitchen. I barely moved.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: So Nadia's the only one with a real alibi for the afternoon.
He meant it as something close to a compliment. A relief. One person who could be cleanly accounted for.
Nadia looked at her mug and said nothing.
